8.topics:
◆ Marks: 5 | Easy
◆ Topics: Closure, Commutative, Associative & Distributive laws. Identity and Inverse.
◆ Tip: Practice 'Finding 10 rational numbers between two fractions'—it's a guaranteed question.

◆ Marks: 8 | Medium
◆ Topics: Equations with variables on both sides, reducing equations to simpler forms.
◆ Tip: Focus on word problems involving age, currency denominations, and perimeter.

◆ Marks: 7 | Medium
◆ Topics: Polygon angle sum, properties of Parallelogram, Rhombus, Rectangle, and Square.
◆ Tip: Learn the 'Exterior Angle Sum' property; it helps solve complex figure problems quickly.

◆ Marks: 6 | Easy
◆ Topics: Grouped data, Histograms, and Pie Charts. Basic Probability concepts.
◆ Tip: Practice drawing Pie charts with a protractor. Probability of 'getting a prime number' on a die is common.

◆ Marks: 6 | Medium
◆ Topics: Pythagorean triplets, Square properties, Long Division Method, and Decimals.
◆ Tip: Memorize squares up to 25. Long division of decimals is a 3-mark favorite for teachers.

◆ Marks: 5 | Easy
◆ Topics: Cube properties, Prime Factorisation, and Estimation method.
◆ Tip: Master the method of 'What smallest number should be multiplied/divided to make a perfect cube'.

◆ Marks: 8 | Hard
◆ Topics: Ratios, Percentages, Profit/Loss, GST, and Compound Interest (CI).
◆ Tip: Focus on CI compounded half-yearly. It's the most confusing yet high-weightage part.

◆ Marks: 8 | Medium
◆ Topics: Addition/Subtraction of polynomials, multiplication, and the 3 Standard Identities.
◆ Tip: Use (a+b)(a-b) = a²-b² for fast numerical calculations like 98 x 102.

◆ Marks: 10 | Hard
◆ Topics: Area of Trapezium/General Quadrilateral. TSA, LSA, and Volume of Cube, Cuboid, Cylinder.
◆ Tip: Always write units (cm²/m³). Surface area of a 'hollow pipe' is a high-level exam question.

◆ Marks: 5 | Medium
◆ Topics: Negative exponents, Laws of Exponents, and Standard Form (Scientific Notation).
◆ Tip: Practice converting 0.000000123 into standard form; it’s a 2-mark fixed question.

◆ Marks: 6 | Medium
◆ Topics: Direct Variation vs Inverse Variation. Time-Work and Speed-Distance.
◆ Tip: In Inverse Proportion, x1*y1 = x2*y2. Use this for 'food lasting for soldiers' problems.

◆ Marks: 8 | Hard
◆ Topics: Common factors, Regrouping, Identities, and Splitting the middle term.
◆ Tip: Splitting the middle term (x²+bx+c) is vital for Class 9/10. Practice at least 20 such sums.

◆ Marks: 5 | Easy
◆ Topics: Bar graphs, Linear graphs, Coordinates (x, y), and independent/dependent variables.
◆ Tip: Practice 'Time-Distance' graphs. Remember: Independent variable always goes on the X-axis.

9.topics:
◆ Marks: 8 | Medium
◆ Topics: Rationalisation, Laws of Exponents, and Decimal expansions (Terminating/Recurring).
◆ Tip: Representing √2 or √3 on the number line using a compass is a 4-mark essential.

◆ Marks: 12 | Hard
◆ Topics: Zeroes, Remainder Theorem, Factor Theorem, and Identities like (x+y+z)².
◆ Tip: Practice factorizing cubic polynomials using the Factor Theorem and long division.

◆ Marks: 4 | Easy
◆ Topics: Cartesian Plane, Quadrants, Abscissa, and Ordinate.
◆ Tip: Don't confuse (x,y) with (y,x). Origin (0,0) is the meeting point of both axes.

◆ Marks: 6 | Medium
◆ Topics: Standard form ax+by+c=0 and plotting straight-line graphs.
◆ Tip: Every linear equation in two variables has infinitely many solutions. Find 4 for a perfect graph.

◆ Marks: 2 | Easy
◆ Topics: Axioms, Postulates, and Definitions.
◆ Tip: Memorize the 5th Postulate (Parallel Postulate) word-for-word; it's often asked in exams.

◆ Marks: 7 | Medium
◆ Topics: Parallel lines, Transversal properties, and Triangle Angle Sum Theorem.
◆ Tip: The Exterior Angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of two opposite interior angles.

◆ Marks: 8 | Hard
◆ Topics: Congruence rules (SAS, ASA, SSS, RHS) and CPCT properties.
◆ Tip: Focus on proving the 'Angles opposite to equal sides are equal' theorem.

◆ Marks: 8 | Medium
◆ Topics: Properties of Parallelograms and the Mid-point Theorem.
◆ Tip: The Mid-point Theorem is the most important proof in this chapter. Don't skip it!

◆ Marks: 8 | Hard
◆ Topics: Equal chords, angle subtended at center, and Cyclic Quadrilaterals.
◆ Tip: Practice the theorem: 'Angle subtended by an arc at the center is double the angle at the circumference'.

◆ Marks: 4 | Easy
◆ Topics: Semi-perimeter(s) and Area of Triangle calculation.
◆ Tip: Use this formula when heights are not given. Perfect for finding areas of scalene triangles.

◆ Marks: 10 | Hard
◆ Topics: CSA, TSA, and Volume of Cone, Sphere, and Hemisphere.
◆ Tip: Ratio problems (e.g., if radius is doubled, what happens to volume?) are very common.

◆ Marks: 5 | Medium
◆ Topics: Histograms with varying base widths and Frequency Polygons.
◆ Tip: In histograms, if class sizes are unequal, calculate adjusted frequency first.

10.topics:
◆ Marks: 6 | Medium
◆ Topics: Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic and proving irrationality (√2, √3).
◆ Tip: Proving '√5 is irrational' is a fixed 3-mark question every year.

◆ Marks: 4 | Easy
◆ Topics: Relationship between Zeroes and Coefficients (α+β, αβ).
◆ Tip: Focus on finding a quadratic polynomial when zeroes or their sum/product are given.

◆ Marks: 6 | Medium
◆ Topics: Substitution, Elimination, and Graphical methods. Consistency conditions.
◆ Tip: Solve 'Upstream and Downstream' boat problems; they carry 5 marks in the board exams.

◆ Marks: 6 | Hard
◆ Topics: Quadratic Formula, Nature of Roots (Discriminant), and Word Problems.
◆ Tip: If D > 0 (roots are real/distinct), if D = 0 (roots are equal), if D < 0 (no real roots).

◆ Marks: 7 | Medium
◆ Topics: n-th term (an) and Sum of n terms (Sn).
◆ Tip: Word problems on 'saving money' or 'seating arrangements' are high-priority applications.

◆ Marks: 8 | Hard
◆ Topics: BPT (Thales Theorem) and Similarity Criteria (AA, SAS).
◆ Tip: The proof of Basic Proportionality Theorem (BPT) is a 5-mark favorite.

◆ Marks: 6 | Medium
◆ Topics: Distance Formula, Section Formula, and Mid-point Formula.
◆ Tip: Practice finding the 'Trisection points' of a line segment using the 1:2 and 2:1 ratios.

◆ Marks: 8 | Hard
◆ Topics: Trig Ratios, Table values (0-90°), and Identities like sin²θ + cos²θ = 1.
◆ Tip: Master the values of tan 30°, 45°, and 60°—they are used in almost every problem.

◆ Marks: 4 | Hard
◆ Topics: Heights and Distances, Elevation and Depression angles.
◆ Tip: Draw the diagram correctly; a wrong diagram means zero marks even if the math is right.

◆ Marks: 4 | Medium
◆ Topics: Tangents from an external point and perpendicularity theorems.
◆ Tip: Prove that 'Tangents from an external point are equal'—it's a 3-mark staple.

◆ Marks: 4 | Medium
◆ Topics: Area of Sector and Segment. Shaded region problems.
◆ Tip: Segment Area = Sector Area - Triangle Area. Practice for θ = 60°, 90°, and 120°.

◆ Marks: 6 | Hard
◆ Topics: Combination of Solids (e.g., Cylinder + 2 Hemispheres).
◆ Tip: When a solid is melted and recast, its VOLUME remains the same. Use this to find new dimensions.

◆ Marks: 7 | Medium
◆ Topics: Mean (Assumed Mean), Median, and Mode. Missing Frequency problems.
◆ Tip: If 'Median' is given, use the formula to find missing 'x' and 'y'—usually 5 marks.

◆ Marks: 4 | Easy
◆ Topics: Playing Cards, Dice, and Coin outcomes.
◆ Tip: Total cards = 52. Remember: 26 Red, 26 Black. 12 Face cards (King, Queen, Jack).

11.topics:
◆ Marks: 6 | Easy
◆ Topics: Power sets, Venn Diagrams, Union, Intersection, and Complement.
◆ Tip: Practice De Morgan’s Laws and the formula for n(A U B U C).

◆ Marks: 6 | Medium
◆ Topics: Cartesian products, Domain, Range, and Special functions (Modulus, Signum).
◆ Tip: Learn to find the domain and range of square root and rational functions.

◆ Marks: 10 | Hard
◆ Topics: Radian measure, sin(x+y), cos(x+y) formulas, and General Solutions.
◆ Tip: Memorize the 'Transformation Formulas' (2 sin A cos B etc.) to simplify complex integrations later.

◆ Marks: 6 | Medium
◆ Topics: Modulus, Conjugate, Iota (i) powers, and Polar representation.
◆ Tip: Focus on finding the 'Argument' (θ) carefully based on the quadrant.

◆ Marks: 5 | Medium
◆ Topics: Shading feasible regions on a graph and algebraic solutions.
◆ Tip: When multiplying/dividing by a negative number, the inequality sign MUST flip.

◆ Marks: 7 | Hard
◆ Topics: nPr, nCr, and Fundamental Principle of Counting.
◆ Tip: Use P for Arrangement (order matters) and C for Selection (order doesn't matter).

◆ Marks: 5 | Medium
◆ Topics: General term (Tr+1) and expansion properties.
◆ Tip: Practice finding the 'Term independent of x' and the 'Middle Term'.

◆ Marks: 7 | Medium
◆ Topics: Arithmetic Mean (AM), GP sum to n terms, and Infinite GP.
◆ Tip: Relationship between AM and GM (AM ≥ GM) is used in many competitive problems.

◆ Marks: 6 | Medium
◆ Topics: Slope-intercept form, Normal form, and distance between parallel lines.
◆ Tip: Angle between two lines formula: tan θ = |(m2-m1)/(1+m1m2)|.

◆ Marks: 6 | Hard
◆ Topics: Equations of Circle, Parabola, Ellipse, and Hyperbola.
◆ Tip: Practice finding Eccentricity (e), Foci, and Directrix for all shapes.

◆ Marks: 4 | Easy
◆ Topics: Octants and distance formula in 3D space.
◆ Tip: Finding the centroid of a triangle in 3D is a simple 2-mark question.

◆ Marks: 10 | Hard
◆ Topics: Standard limits and Differentiation using the First Principle.
◆ Tip: Mastery of sinx/x = 1 limit and Product/Quotient rules is mandatory for Class 12.

◆ Marks: 6 | Medium
◆ Topics: Variance and Standard Deviation (SD).
◆ Tip: Coefficient of Variation (CV) helps in comparing the stability of two data sets.

◆ Marks: 6 | Medium
◆ Topics: Axiomatic probability and Addition theorem.
◆ Tip: Focus on P(A or B) and Mutually Exclusive vs Exhaustive events.

12.topics:
◆ Marks: 5 | Medium
◆ Topics: Reflexive, Symmetric, Transitive, and Equivalence relations. Bijective functions.
◆ Tip: Proving a function is 'One-to-One and Onto' is a fixed board question.

◆ Marks: 3 | Medium
◆ Topics: Principal Value Branches (PVB) and domain/range restrictions.
◆ Tip: Principal value of sin⁻¹(-1/2) or cos⁻¹(-1/2) must stay within the PVB range.

◆ Marks: 5 | Easy
◆ Topics: Matrix multiplication and Symmetric/Skew-symmetric properties.
◆ Tip: Remember: (AB)ᵀ = BᵀAᵀ. This property is frequently used in 1-mark MCQs.

◆ Marks: 5 | Medium
◆ Topics: Adjoint, Inverse, and Solving equations using Matrix Method (AX=B).
◆ Tip: Solving 3 equations using A⁻¹ is a confirmed 5-mark question.

◆ Marks: 9 | Hard
◆ Topics: Chain Rule, Logarithmic differentiation, and Second-order derivatives.
◆ Tip: If a function is differentiable, it is ALWAYS continuous. Use this for 'k' value problems.

◆ Marks: 6 | Hard
◆ Topics: Rate of change, Intervals of Inc/Dec, and Maxima/Minima.
◆ Tip: 6-mark word problems on 'Cylinder inside a Sphere' or 'Rectangular tank' are crucial.

◆ Marks: 10 | Very Hard
◆ Topics: Substitution, Partial Fractions, and Definite Integral Properties.
◆ Tip: King's Property (P4) - replacing x with (a+b-x) solves 80% of tricky definite integrals.

◆ Marks: 6 | Hard
◆ Topics: Area under simple curves (Circles, Parabolas) using integration.
◆ Tip: Always draw a rough sketch and find limits before integrating. Shaded area is key.

◆ Topics: Order/Degree and Integrating Factor (IF) for Linear equations.
◆ Tip: Solution of dy/dx + Py = Q is y(IF) = ∫Q(IF)dx + C. Very important!

◆ Marks: 6 | Medium
◆ Topics: Dot product, Cross product, and Projection of vectors.
◆ Tip: Dot product = Scalar (a.b), Cross product = Vector (a x b). Area of Triangle = 1/2 |a x b|.

◆ Marks: 8 | Hard
◆ Topics: Direction Cosines and Shortest Distance between skew lines.
◆ Tip: The formula for Shortest Distance between lines is a guaranteed 4-5 mark question.

◆ Marks: 5 | Medium
◆ Topics: Feasible Region and Corner Point method.
◆ Tip: Draw clear graphs. Max/Min value always occurs at the corner points of the shaded area.

◆ Marks: 8 | Hard
◆ Topics: Conditional Probability, Bayes' Theorem, and Bernoulli trials.
◆ Tip: Bayes' Theorem is the most important topic. Practice 'Bag 1 and Bag 2' ball problems.
